perturbation.original_text: 5 The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that any breach by it of any of the provisions of this Agreement may cause serious damage to the Disclosing Party. In particular (but without limitation) it is recognised that parts o f the Confidential Information may be patent able or capable of being the subject of registered design rights or similar protection and that premature disclosure thereof may prejudice the ability of the disclosing party to obtain such protection. The Rece iving Party undertakes fully and effectively to indemnify and keep so indemnified the Disclosing Party for and against all loss, damage, costs and liabilities suffered or incurred by it arising from: - 5.1 the unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informatio n by the Receiving Party and any person to whom disclosure of such Confidential Information is permitted under Clause 2 of this Agreement; or 5.2 a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5 The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that any breach by it of any of the provisions of this Agreement may cause serious damage to the Disclosing Party. In particular (but without limitation) it is recognised that parts o f the Confidential Information may be patent able or capable of being the subject of registered design rights or similar protection and that premature disclosure thereof may prejudice the ability of the disclosing party to obtain such protection. The Receiving Party will only be responsible for grossly negligent or willful misconduct regarding Confidential Information. The Rece iving Party undertakes fully and effectively to indemnify and keep so indemnified the Disclosing Party for and against all loss, damage, costs and liabilities suffered or incurred by it arising from: - 5.1 the unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informatio n by the Receiving Party and any person to whom disclosure of such Confidential Information is permitted under Clause 2 of this Agreement; or 5.2 a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in Section 5 introduces a standard of liability ('grossly negligent or willful misconduct') that contradicts the subsequent indemnity clause, which holds the Receiving Party fully liable for 'any breach' including unauthorized disclosure. This creates an in-text contradiction. The Receiving Party is only responsible for 'grossly negligent or willful misconduct' then they are still responsible for any breach of contract. Which creates an uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5 The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges that any breach by it of any of the provisions of this Agreement may cause serious damage to the Disclosing Party. In particular (but without limitation) it is recognised that parts o f the Confidential Information may be patent able or capable of being the subject of registered design rights or similar protection and that premature disclosure thereof may prejudice the ability of the disclosing party to obtain such protection. The Receiving Party will only be responsible for grossly negligent or willful misconduct regarding Confidential Information. The Rece iving Party undertakes fully and effectively to indemnify and keep so indemnified the Disclosing Party for and against all loss, damage, costs and liabilities suffered or incurred by it arising from: - 5.1 the unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informatio n by the Receiving Party and any person to whom disclosure of such Confidential Information is permitted under Clause 2 of this Agreement; or 5.2 a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement. The Parties acknowledge that damages wi ll not normally be an adequate remedy for breach of any of the terms set out in this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party should be entitled to equitable relief including injunctions in respect of any breach by the Receiving Party. Any failure by the D isclosing Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall not, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof, preclude any exercise of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'grossly negligent or willful misconduct' standard for the Receiving Party's responsibility regarding Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the subsequent indemnity clause (5.1 and 5.2) within the same section, which holds the Receiving Party fully liable for 'any breach' including unauthorized disclosure, regardless of negligence or willful misconduct. This creates a clear and impactful in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party cannot be held responsible only for 'grossly negligent or willful misconduct' while simultaneously being fully responsible for 'any breach'. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8 The construction, validity and performance of th is Agreement shall be governed by English Law and the Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 The construction, validity and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by English Law, except for matters relating to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware, USA. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England for all disputes except those relating to intellectual property, for which they submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 8 introduces a split jurisdiction clause, specifying that intellectual property disputes are governed by Delaware law and adjudicated in Delaware courts, while all other disputes remain under English law and jurisdiction. This creates an in-text contradiction by establishing two different legal systems for the same agreement. This uncertainty makes contract enforcements more difficult and makes the contract less clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8 The construction, validity and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by English Law, except for matters relating to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware, USA. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England for all disputes except those relating to intellectual property, for which they submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Delaware. 9 This Agreement shall become effective on the date first written above. 10 The Parties obligations under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of five (5) years from the date hereof, save that the obligations of the parties as receiving parties under Clause 6 of this Agreement shall continue thereafter until fully discharged by performance. IN WITNESS WHEREO F the Parties have caused this Agreement to be executed by their duly authorised representatives as of the day and year first written above. Date:___________________ Date:___________________ Signed for and on behalf of Signed for and on behalf of Microkerf Limited _______________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states that English Law governs the entire agreement and all disputes fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of English courts. The modified text creates an exception, stating that intellectual property matters are governed by Delaware law and fall under the jurisdiction of Delaware courts. This directly contradicts the original all-encompassing statement about English law and jurisdiction. Since the Contradicted Text and Changed Text are identical, it further shows that the initial change caused a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
